summary: PR 2522 split the previously broad Crazy Hand init translation unit into many per-action ftCrazyHand source/header files, updated GALE01 split ranges, and changed configure.py so the new Crazy Hand objects are treated as Matching. The bot report shows a net increase in matched, linked code and data, while many apparent broken matches are the old ftCh_Init symbols moving into newly split units.

key_files.role: Expanded one ftCrazyHand split into many per-action text/data/sdata2 ranges from ftCh_Init through ftCh_TagCancel.
Changed Crazy Hand build objects from a single NonMatching ftCh_Init.c object to many Matching ftCrazyHand objects.
Shrank the old monolithic Crazy Hand unit; changed-files metadata records 30 additions and 2767 deletions.
Collapsed old broad header declarations; changed-files metadata records 1 addition and 228 deletions.

Added new per-action Crazy Hand implementation files, including Wait1_0, FingerBeam, GrabUnk1_B174, Poke, Slam, Damage_0, TagCancel, RockCrush, Sweep/Walk, BackAirplane, capture, throw, and related action units.
Added matching per-action headers with address-commented prototypes and GALE01 address-style include guards.
Touched motion-state registration surface for Crazy Hand linkage; exact diff not loaded.
One-line include or linkage adjustment; exact diff not loaded.
One-line include or linkage adjustment; exact diff not loaded.

One-line include or linkage adjustment; exact diff not loaded.

what_changed.change: Introduced many new Crazy Hand translation units and headers under src/melee/ft/chara/ftCrazyHand.
Updated config/GALE01/splits.txt so Crazy Hand is split across per-action units with explicit .text and .sdata2 ranges, and .data ranges where present.
Updated configure.py from Object(NonMatching, ftCh_Init.c) to Object(Matching, ...) entries for ftCh_Init.c plus each new Crazy Hand unit.
Added headers and includes to wire functions across Crazy Hand, Common, Master Hand, and fighter source files.

Bot report recorded +1172 matched-code bytes, +24328 linked-code bytes, +2416 matched-data bytes, +2432 linked-data bytes, and 293 new matches. It also reported 254 broken matches in old ftCh_Init and one unmatched-item regression, consistent with symbols moving out of the original monolithic unit.

smart_moves.text: Split a large Crazy Hand unit at section boundaries and immediately reflected those boundaries in both splits.txt and configure.py.
Kept many not-yet-semantic symbols in address-style names such as ftCh_Init_801592D4 and fn_801588B8, avoiding unsupported semantic renames.
Created narrow headers per action file, making cross-TU dependencies explicit after the split.

decomp_lessons.lesson: When a large matched file is split into new translation units, expect bot reports to show many broken matches in the old unit and corresponding new matches in the new units; evaluate the net report and unit movement rather than reading old-unit broken matches alone as regressions.
evidence_refs: text_corpus.jsonl:bot report new matches and broken matches

decomp_lessons.lesson: For character action splits, update split metadata and build object classification together; the PR changed both GALE01 split ranges and configure.py Matching object entries for the same ftCrazyHand units.
Use per-action headers to preserve prototypes after splitting a fighter action TU, and add includes at call sites rather than relying on the former monolithic header.

naming_conventions.convention: Crazy Hand action files use ftCh_<Action>.c and ftCh_<Action>.h names, for example ftCh_BackAirplane1.c, ftCh_FingerBeam.c, ftCh_TagCancel.c, and ftCh_Wait1_0.c.
Headers in the loaded excerpt use GALE01_<address> include guards and address-commented prototypes.
Callbacks are named with action suffixes such as _Anim, _IASA, _Phys, and _Coll where semantics are clear; initializers may remain address-style ftCh_Init_<addr>.
Some unknown functions remain fn_<address>, as shown in the bot report, preserving uncertainty rather than inventing names.

assembly_or_matching_tactics.tactic: Split the original ftCh_Init address range into contiguous per-action .text ranges and matching .sdata2 ranges in splits.txt.
Promote each new ftCrazyHand object to Matching in configure.py after the split.
Use narrow include additions to resolve cross-file references created by the split, such as adding ftCrazyHand/ftCh_TagCancel.h to ftCo_Fall.c.

One loaded function contains an unnamed padding/local stack array, `u8 _[8];`, in ftCh_BackAirplane3_Anim. The slice does not include author explanation, so this should be treated as a matching tactic candidate rather than a general style lesson.

review_feedback.author: decomp-dev[bot]
review_feedback.summary: Reported GALE01 progress: matched code 67.77% (+0.03%, +1172 bytes), linked code 39.86% (+0.63%, +24328 bytes), matched data 39.65% (+0.20%, +2416 bytes), linked data 33.46% (+0.20%, +2432 bytes), 293 new matches, 254 broken matches, and 1 regression in an unmatched item.

follow_up_queries: PR 2522 Crazy Hand split broken matches old ftCh_Init new matches

notes: PR body is empty, so intent is inferred only from title, diff, changed-file metadata, and bot report.
The raw diff is truncated in loaded_files, so many exact one-line changes and later file contents are known only from changed-files metadata, not exact hunks.
There are no inline review comments. The approval has no body, so no reviewer rationale should be inferred.
Bot report supports progress and movement of matches, but this intake did not independently run builds or objdiff.

accepted_candidate_records.title: Treat old-unit broken matches carefully during TU splits
accepted_candidate_records.text: In PR 2522, splitting ftCrazyHand out of a monolithic ftCh_Init.c caused the bot report to show many broken matches in the old ftCh_Init unit alongside corresponding new matches in new per-action units. For split PRs, compare old-unit losses with new-unit gains and split metadata before classifying report noise as a regression.

accepted_candidate_records.title: Keep splits.txt and configure.py synchronized for matched TU splits
accepted_candidate_records.text: PR 2522 updated GALE01 split ranges and configure.py Matching object entries together when splitting Crazy Hand into per-action files.

source_update_candidates.title: Crazy Hand per-action source files under src/melee/ft/chara/ftCrazyHand
source_update_candidates.text: PR 2522 split the Crazy Hand code from the broad ftCh_Init.c range into many per-action ftCh_*.c and ftCh_*.h files, with configure.py marking them Matching and config/GALE01/splits.txt assigning contiguous text/sdata2 ranges.
source_update_candidates.reason: This is a path/split ownership fact candidate supported by the PR diff, but it should be curated into source-owned path facts rather than treated as a global decomp lesson.

source_update_candidates.title: Document report interpretation for translation-unit split PRs
source_update_candidates.text: When a PR splits a matched TU, bot reports can show old-unit broken matches and new-unit matches for the same code movement. Reviewers should reconcile split metadata and net movement before treating the old-unit losses as regressions.
source_update_candidates.reason: PR evidence supports a reusable caution, but whether to add it to global standards is a curator decision.

rejection_notes: Do not infer that every new ftCrazyHand function was manually verified by the author; the slice contains bot report evidence but no author validation notes.
Do not promote `u8 _[8];` stack padding from one loaded function into accepted style without objdiff/build rationale.
Do not infer reviewer intent beyond an approval with an empty body.
Do not treat the many old ftCh_Init broken matches as standalone regressions without considering the split movement shown by the diff and bot report.
